Table of Contents

Interface ITableSort

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.Geodatabase
Assembly
ESRI.Server.Geodatabase.dll

Provides access to members that return and modify information to sort a table.

[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
[Guid("2D0F935E-32F5-11D2-A06F-0000F8775BF9")]
public interface ITableSort

Field sort order.

bool Ascending[string Field] { set; }

Parameters

Field string

Property Value

bool

CaseSensitive[string]

Character fields case sensitive. Default: False.

bool CaseSensitive[string Field] { set; }

Parameters

Field string

Property Value

bool

Sample Snippet

Compare

Compare call back interface. Specify Null (default) for normal behavior.

ITableSortCallBack Compare { set; }

Property Value

ITableSortCallBack

Examples

Sample Snippet

Cursor

The cursor of the data to sort on. Ensure that sorting fields are available. Cancels SelectionSet.

ICursor Cursor { set; }

Property Value

ICursor

Examples

Sample Snippet

Fields

Comma list of field names to sort on.

string Fields { set; }

Property Value

string

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>Each table in a database has an ordered collection of fields, there is always at least one field in a table. The ordered collection behaves like a list, so it is possible to access individual fields by a numbered position (or index) in the list.</P> <P>A <STRONG>Fields </STRONG>collection can also exist independently of a table, for example when creating an index on a table you will need to define which fields take part in the index. There are many interfaces which either require or return a <STRONG>Fields </STRONG>collection; these include <STRONG>IClass </STRONG>(therefore also <STRONG>IObjectClass </STRONG>and <STRONG>IFeatureClass</STRONG>), <STRONG>ICursor</STRONG>, <STRONG>IIndex</STRONG>, <STRONG>IFeatureClassDraw</STRONG>, <STRONG>IRowBuffer</STRONG>, <STRONG>ISimpleDataConverter</STRONG>, <STRONG>ITableSort </STRONG>and <STRONG>IValidate</STRONG>.</P>

IDByIndex[int]

A id by its index value.

long IDByIndex[int Index] { get; }

Parameters

Index int

Property Value

long

List of sorted IDs.

IEnumIDs IDs { get; }

IEnumIDs

IQueryFilter QueryFilter { get; set; }

IQueryFilter

Rows

Cursor of sorted rows.

ICursor Rows { get; }

ICursor

SelectionSet

The selection set as a source of the data to sort on. Cancels Cursor.

ISelectionSet SelectionSet { get; set; }

Property Value

ISelectionSet

Examples

Sample Snippet

SortCharacters[string]

Number of characters to sort on, for string fields. A null (default) sorts on the whole string.

int SortCharacters[string Field] { set; }

Parameters

Field string

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

Table

The table as a source of the data to sort on.

ITable Table { get; set; }

Property Value

ITable

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>A <STRONG>Table </STRONG>object has one or more columns, referred to as fields, and contains an unordered collection of rows. Each row has for each field exactly one value in the data type of the field.</P> <P>A <STRONG>Table </STRONG>is a <STRONG>Dataset </STRONG>and properties such as the name of the table, the persistable name object for the table, and the workspace containing the table, may be obtained via the <STRONG>IDataset </STRONG>interface. In relational terms, a <STRONG>Table </STRONG>object represents an RDBMS table or view. In objected-oriented terms, a Table object represents an ObjectClass or a RelationshipClass in a geodatabase. A <STRONG>Table </STRONG>object hands out <STRONG>Row </STRONG>objects which themselves support application callable methods depending on the type of data stored in the table.</P> <P>The <STRONG>Name </STRONG>property of a <STRONG>Table</STRONG>, accessible via the <STRONG>IDataset </STRONG>interface, returns its fully qualified name. The level of qualification may vary depending on the host DBMS. For example, a table named ‘pipes’, owned by a user named ‘gas’ may be called ‘pipes’ in and Access or File Geodatabase, ‘gas.pipes’ on Oracle and ‘mydb.gas.pipes’ on SQLServer. The <STRONG>ParseTableNamemethod </STRONG>on the <STRONG>ISqlSyntax </STRONG>interface supported by the table’s workspace can be used to split the fully qualified name into its components.</P>

Methods

Sort(ITrackCancel) 

void Sort(ITrackCancel TrackCancel)

TrackCancel ITrackCancel
